title: adsbtrack
description_html: Historical ADS-B trace extraction. Browse tables directly or use the canned queries below to explore analytical signal surfaced by the extractor.

emergency_flights.title: Emergency-flagged flights
emergency_flights.description: Flights that tripped an emergency signal: a 7500 / 7600 / 7700 squawk recorded in emergency_squawk, or a non-null emergency_flag (e.g. nordo). These are rare per aircraft; the query lists individual events rather than monthly aggregates.
emergency_flights.sql:
SELECT
  icao,
  takeoff_time,
  landing_time,
  callsign,
  emergency_squawk,
  emergency_flag,
  round(duration_minutes, 1) AS duration_min,
  mission_type
FROM flights
WHERE emergency_flag IS NOT NULL
   OR emergency_squawk IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY takeoff_time DESC
LIMIT 100

top_aircraft_this_year.title: Top 10 aircraft by flight count this year
top_aircraft_this_year.description: Most active hexes in the current calendar year, with registration / type / operator joined from aircraft_registry when available.
top_aircraft_this_year.sql:
SELECT
  f.icao,
  r.registration,
  r.type_code,
  r.owner_operator,
  COUNT(*) AS flights,
  MIN(f.takeoff_date) AS first_seen,
  MAX(f.takeoff_date) AS last_seen
FROM flights f
LEFT JOIN aircraft_registry r USING (icao)
WHERE substr(f.takeoff_time, 1, 4) = strftime('%Y', 'now')
GROUP BY f.icao
ORDER BY flights DESC
LIMIT 10

military_activity_last_week.title: Military-registered aircraft activity in the last 7 days
military_activity_last_week.description: Flights whose ICAO hex falls inside a curated military allocation range (see mil_hex_ranges) within the last week. This uses the authoritative hex ranges rather than the mission_type classifier, which does not currently emit a 'military' value.
military_activity_last_week.sql:
SELECT
  f.icao,
  r.registration,
  r.type_code,
  r.owner_operator,
  COUNT(*) AS flights,
  MAX(f.takeoff_time) AS last_flight,
  m.country,
  m.branch
FROM flights f
JOIN mil_hex_ranges m
  ON f.icao >= m.range_start
  AND f.icao <= m.range_end
LEFT JOIN aircraft_registry r USING (icao)
WHERE f.takeoff_time >= datetime('now', '-7 days')
GROUP BY f.icao
ORDER BY flights DESC

flights_with_long_signal_gaps.title: Flights with signal gaps longer than 10 minutes
flights_with_long_signal_gaps.description: Within-flight coverage holes exceeding 10 minutes. Some of these are genuine receiver-coverage gaps (oceans, remote areas); others may indicate the transponder was turned off. A dedicated 'adsbtrack gaps' CLI (planned) will classify these.
flights_with_long_signal_gaps.sql:
SELECT
  icao,
  takeoff_time,
  landing_time,
  round(duration_minutes, 1) AS duration_min,
  signal_gap_count,
  round(signal_gap_secs / 60.0, 1) AS gap_minutes,
  signal_gap_secs
FROM flights
WHERE signal_gap_secs > 600
ORDER BY signal_gap_secs DESC
LIMIT 50

off_airport_landings.title: Aircraft with the most off-airport landings
off_airport_landings.description: Confirmed landings where no nearby airport AND no helipad matched. Common for helicopters landing at private pads that aren't in the helipads table, and for fixed-wing aircraft landing on unregistered strips.
off_airport_landings.sql:
SELECT
  f.icao,
  r.registration,
  r.type_code,
  r.owner_operator,
  COUNT(*) AS off_airport_landings,
  MAX(f.landing_time) AS last_event
FROM flights f
LEFT JOIN aircraft_registry r USING (icao)
WHERE f.landing_type = 'confirmed'
  AND f.destination_icao IS NULL
  AND f.destination_helipad_id IS NULL
GROUP BY f.icao
HAVING off_airport_landings > 0
ORDER BY off_airport_landings DESC
LIMIT 20
